post_title: Playlist for Space Music that is emotive, moving and encaptivating
post_body:
Currently assembling a playlist with 'space music' that really scratches a specific itch for me. Only a few artists have ever given me the right 'combination' of melody, rhythm, ambient vibes, space sounds, etc, that really does it for me. But when it does, it is magical and the best type of music there is out there. For me at least.  
So it needs an emotive melody, a beat / drums (but no 4/4 dance beat, but more like groovy drums), space sounds, synth pads, etc. 

Certain artists which do it for me and are thus featured in this playlist:

\- Carbon Based Lifeforms  
\- Stellardrone  
\- Epigenetics  
\- Solar Fields  
\- Astronaut Ape  
\- Data Rebel

It's only really the first three which have the perfect vibes for me. I can't really explain what it is.  
Too many tracks of this 'psybient' genre are too much 'beat' and too few melody, or they have the melodies but too few space sounds or are too generic.  
I don't like long 'droney' tracks either. Nor generic 'synthwave'.

So if you know what I am aiming at, I would love to hear your recommendations. Artist name + track name would be sweet. Thanks!

If this appeals to you and you want to check out the playlist you can search on Spotify:  
'Music For Space Travellers'  
(not allowed to link here)

Thanks!
comments: Michael Stearns’ Planetary Unfolding is easily the best in the genre, in my opinion. Its emotional, the sound stage is absolutely massive, it’s captivating, and has a very classical feel
Sun electric – 30.7.94 gets an honorable mention as well
Yep, my favorite space music album of all time. From 1981!

Ever listen to Telomere?

https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKsCWLymgwsaGHY2yPPUloH4CFZaqAE1F&si=0DHo4EROyx7SAe_r
Yep!  I just posted his discography a few minutes ago.
Incredible album

Just checked that out and listened for about 10 min. Skipped through a bit because I was hoping for some epic melodies or drums to kick in, but that never happened 😄

It's great stuff though. I will definitely listen to it again, properly, in the near future.

It doesn't quite match what I am looking for due to the lack of melody or drums. The sound is definitely there though.

Don't mind me. I am very picky/ specific in what I search for.   
  
This album for instance, almost every track hits the spot:  
[https://youtu.be/c95M49h1P-M](https://youtu.be/c95M49h1P-M)

It's spacy, yet has these synth melodies and grooves that make me forget about my life 😄

Ever listen to Telomere?

https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKsCWLymgwsaGHY2yPPUloH4CFZaqAE1F&si=0DHo4EROyx7SAe_r
State Azure and Martin Sturtzer are bith great.
There are some Martin Sturtzer albums that might fit the bill. Also, check out albums by Transponder.
Transponder does some great stuff!
Richard Burmer: Bhakti Point
One of my all time favourite albums.  The man was a stone cold genius.  Thanks for remembering him.
You could try Hannah Peel's Mary Casio: Journey to Cassiopeia
Add Steve Roach to this list. Structures from Silence is one of the best records ever made.

Every time the drum hits I'm teleported from space to a dance club, unfortunately

Would love this if it wasn't for drums at the end of each track

Right. For me the drums are often needed to get into some kind of 'groove'. But I usually don't like '4/4' beats, that's indeed to 'dancy' for me. 

Perfect drums for me are   
Stellardrone - Light Years  
CBL - Abiogenesis

Just some examples. I can like beatless as well but then there needs to be a good emotive melody. Not just drones and sounds.   
Basically I don't like it when it's just 'background music', it needs to keep my attention.

Brian Eno has said that ambient music “rewards attention but does not demand it,” so you are looking for ambient music that contradicts how it was defined by the man who invented the genre. Doesn’t make you “wrong” or anything like that, but it does put you at odds with the concept, to some degree.
That said, I am checking out your preferred artists. :-)

Well maybe a good question is, what genre is it that I am actually talking about? It certainly has elements of ambient music, but it's not solely that.

For instance the track: Carbon Based Lifeforms - Abiogenesis

What genre is that? Because that's a great example.
The best term I've heard for it is Downtempo
Right. Yea it certainly has elements of Downtempo, but that's still a very 'broad' term that covers so many different types of music still I think.

I think I know what you're getting at, and it's hard to put a name on it. Spacy ambient vibes, bid evolving pads, slow, bass-heavy simple rhythms. Not ambient, not psybient, not ambient dub, not downtempo...

Aside from those bands (and glad to see a fellow Epigenetics enjoyer! His new album is good!), my wife and I really dig this vibe too and we like:

 - Yagya - Rigning
 - HUVA Network - Ephemeris
 - Connect.Ohm - 9980
 - Tineidae - Reports from Isul (buit really almost all his stuff)
 - Ochre - Project Caelus (different, but has that same emotional tie)
 - Martin Roth - Analog guy in a digital world
 - Lauge & AES Dana - Terrene

(Lots of AES Dana and Martin Nonstatic in general)

You may find a lot of good stuff on Ultimae and Tympanik labels. Happy hunting!

Hey. Yes saw the new Epigenetics album on the Psychedelic Muse channel this week. Really good stuff.  
Also nice that you can enjoy this type of music with your wife 😄

There is actually few music like this out there I believe, even though there is a lot that comes close. But is not quite it. 

I will check out all your recommendations for sure. I already know of Connect.Ohm 9980 and know a lot of music of Aes Dana, but the rest is new to me. So thanks for that!
One half of Connect.Ohm is the band Cell (the other Hybrid Leisureland), his albums are good but his live album Live in Corfu is excellent, check that out too if you haven't!
Oh didn't know that! Although I have a track or two of Cell in the playlist already 😄

try Save Crystal 
https://open.spotify.com/track/260VfAZd3BSqEEFlNtSA8C?si=vUtYtK4VTPaCjF7unJSlBQ

Was gonna shameless plug then saw ‘no long droney tracks’ 

I will respectfully withhold.

Hope the playlist is coming along well!
I’d be interested in hearing those!

I know Blood Incantation is mainly a death metal band but they have an incredible spacy ambient release called Timewave Zero

Another one that I really like is Andrew Huang’s Blue Dawn

Here’s an artist you can try, Telomere.  I bought these on cd, but he’s on Apple Music.  And Spotify.

Telomere \[1998\] Astral Currents  
Telomere \[2001\] Zoetosis  
Telomere \[2007\] The Stellar Sea  
Telomere \[2012\] Lux Primordia  
Telomere \[2018\] Supergiant

Asura- Galaxies Part 1

Have you checked out the French band Redshift or Cosmic Ground?

Hey thanks. Asura - Galaxies Part 1 is already in the playlist haha.

Yes it is very nice. It doesn't reach the same level for me as CBL in terms of emotive impact, maybe it's the lack of melodies. But the space sound and epicness are certainly there.

I have not heard of Redshift / Cosmic Ground so I will check those out! Thank you.
Both have an early Tangerine Dream vibe, I love them both.
totally random but just got tuned into [this](https://www.reddit.com/r/Vaporwave/comments/1t580xa/e3d16a454055c7875642d2a54234/)
Pretty nice! However, I miss the melodies and the groovy drums 😄
Are Parallax-5 and S1gns Of L1fe too much beat/too psybient?

Hey. Great recommendations! I just checked some tracks of s1gnsofl1fe and they are awesome. Also the first track of Parallax-5 was immediately nice. Will definitely check out more of them. It has the 'melodicness' I am looking for for sure.
Loraine James, "The Perception of Me"
& any of the "synth" tracks on Emily A. Sprague's 'Mount Vision' album, especially "Synth 3"
I made this a few years ago in case it’s of interest https://open.spotify.com/playlist/4ccIsyHkz0ZWzl1HB4Slba?si=m9mXrD4nRCyz9agoW1Lyhg

Pretty cool! Most of the tracks on their don't seem to have the melodies and drums / progression that I am looking for. But I was very pleased to see GAS - Microscopic on the list. One of my long term favorites. Going to add it to my playlist now 😄
Damn that first track in the playlist is sick. I'm definitely listening to the rest of it. It makes me wonder if the Subnautica soundtrack might fit the bill. It reminds me of "Into the Unknown"

Cool! Yea Epigenetics has some sick tunes and beats. Definitely one of my favorite artists of the recent years.

I am going to check out the Subnautica soundtrack. Now that you mention it, most of the music in my list is kind of fitting as a soundtrack for movies or games or so.
